input.initialPrompt: Analyze this project for incremental build issues. Identify any custom targets that would unnecessarily re-run on every build even when nothing has changed.

data.reasoningText: Let me analyze the project files to identify incremental build issues.

toolResult.textResultForLlm:
1. <Project Sdk="Microsoft.NET.Sdk">
2.   <PropertyGroup>
3.     <TargetFramework>net8.0</TargetFramework>
4.   </PropertyGroup>
5. 
6.   <Target Name="GenerateTimestamp" BeforeTargets="CoreCompile">
7.     <PropertyGroup>
8.       <TimestampFile>$(IntermediateOutputPath)build-timestamp.txt</TimestampFile>
9.     </PropertyGroup>
10.     <WriteLinesToFile File="$(TimestampFile)" Lines="Built at: $([System.DateTime]::Now.ToString())" Overwrite="true" />
11.   </Target>
12. 
13.   <Target Name="EmbedGitHash" BeforeTargets="CoreCompile">
14.     <Exec Command="git rev-parse HEAD" ConsoleToMsBuild="true" IgnoreExitCode="true">
15.       <Output TaskParameter="ConsoleOutput" PropertyName="GitHash" />
16.     </Exec>
17.     <WriteLinesToFile File="$(IntermediateOutputPath)git-hash.g.cs"
18.                      Lines="namespace IncrementalBroken { internal static class BuildInfo { public const string GitHash = "$(GitHash)"%3B } }"
19.                      Overwrite="true" />
20.     <ItemGroup>
21.       <Compile Include="$(IntermediateOutputPath)git-hash.g.cs" />
22.     </ItemGroup>
23.   </Target>
24. </Project>
25.

data.content:
## Incremental Build Issues Found

Both custom targets will **re-run on every build** even when nothing has changed, because they lack MSBuild's `Inputs`/`Outputs` attributes that enable up-to-date checks.

### 1. `GenerateTimestamp` (line 6)
- Writes a file containing `DateTime.Now` on every build.
- The timestamp **always changes**, so even if you added `Inputs`/`Outputs`, the file content would differ each time, defeating incremental caching and forcing `CoreCompile` to re-run.

### 2. `EmbedGitHash` (line 13)
- Runs `git rev-parse HEAD` and writes a `.g.cs` file **every build**.
- No `Inputs`/`Outputs` declared, so MSBuild cannot skip it.
- Even when the git hash hasn't changed, the file is rewritten (touching its timestamp), which triggers recompilation.

### Why this matters
Every `dotnet build` re-executes both targets, regenerates files, and invalidates the compiler's up-to-date check — resulting in a full recompile even when no source code changed.

### Recommended fixes

**`GenerateTimestamp`** — either remove it or gate it so it only runs in CI/Release:
```xml
<Target Name="GenerateTimestamp" BeforeTargets="CoreCompile"
        Condition="'$(CI)' == 'true'">
```

**`EmbedGitHash`** — add `Inputs`/`Outputs` and use `WriteLinesToFile` only when the content actually changes:
```xml
<Target Name="EmbedGitHash" BeforeTargets="CoreCompile"
        Inputs="$(DotGitDir)\HEAD;$(DotGitDir)\refs\heads\**"
        Outputs="$(IntermediateOutputPath)git-hash.g.cs">
```

This way MSBuild skips the target when the git HEAD hasn't moved, preventing unnecessary recompilation.
